events.title: kidney stones
supporting_evidence: Dehydration increases the risk of kidney stone formation.
High animal protein intake increases the risk of kidney stone formation.
The doctor recommends limiting animal protein intake.
High oxalate intake increases the risk of kidney stone formation.
current_state: Dehydration increases the risk of kidney stone formation.
High animal protein intake increases the risk of kidney stone formation.
The doctor recommends limiting animal protein intake.
High oxalate intake increases the risk of kidney stone formation.
planned_actions: No surgical intervention is recommended at this time.
The patient should consume less than 1,000 milligrams of sodium a day.
The patient should avoid food rich in sodium oxalate.
The patient should reduce the amount of beef.
The patient should reduce the amount of poultry.
care_context: The patient previously experienced blood in the urine due to kidney stones.
The patient has one stone in the left kidney.
The patient admits to not drinking enough water.
